business.headline: Fuel price hike essential, says Montek Singh AhluwaliaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, former Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission Montek Singh Ahluwalia discussed the fuel price hikes, current economic crisis and the need for structural reforms.

Why are 71% of Indians planning fewer outings? What's causing this shiftFor many Indians, a quick drive, weekend outing or casual trip may no longer feel as easy on the pocket as before. As fuel costs continue to rise, households across the country appear to be rethinking how often they step out, but what exactly is driving this change?

Tata Sons board meeting: 3 key things to watch tomorrowThe Tata Sons board is set to meet tomorrow, May 26, at a time when several important questions surround the group's future. Although no formal details have been shared, the outcome of the meeting could be closely watched for hints about the group's next phase of growth.

8th Pay Commission: Why govt may not accept all demands by employee unionsEmployee unions have pressed the 8th Pay Commission for a higher fitment factor, pension changes and a five-family formula. A senior representative said fiscal pressure and long-term liabilities could push the government towards a middle path.

Markets rally over 1% on Iran-US talks as crude oil falls below $100The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,073.61 points, or 1.42%, to settle at 76,488.96, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 250.30 points, or 1.06%, to close at 23,969.60.

Silver jumps over Rs 4,000: Why is it glittering more than gold?Silver prices sparkled in the domestic market on Monday, jumping by more than Rs 4,000 and outperforming gold amid a weaker US dollar, easing crude oil prices and hopes of a possible US-Iran peace deal. But what exactly is making silver shine brighter than gold right now?

India's ethanol boom may soon face an oversupply crisisIndia's ethanol boom helped the country hit E20 early. Now distilleries, oil companies and policymakers are confronting a new question. Has India already built more ethanol capacity than it needs?

Who could become billionaires after SpaceX IPO? Meet Musk's inner circleElon Musk may already be the world's richest man, but a future SpaceX IPO could create a fresh batch of billionaires around him. From top executives to early insiders, several members of Musk's inner circle could see their fortunes soar.

Explained: FM Sitharaman's '3Fs' strategy amid West Asia crisisNirmala Sitharaman said PM Narendra Modi's call to conserve foreign exchange is very important as the Iran crisis strains India's import bill and supply chains.

What kept India's petrol and diesel price hike lower than many nations?As fuel prices surged across several countries during the global oil crisis, India saw relatively smaller increases in petrol and diesel rates. So, what helped keep prices in check despite rising crude oil costs? Let's have a look.

Not sure how to e-verify your ITR? Here are 5 easy ways to do itThink filing your ITR means the job is done? Not quite. Many taxpayers do not realise that after submitting their return, they must also e-verify it. The good part is that you can choose from five different methods, depending on what feels easiest for you.

Cheaper crude won't reduce your petrol, diesel expenses anytime soonGlobal crude oil prices have slipped below $100 a barrel, but petrol and diesel prices in India continue to rise. The gap reflects earlier oil company losses, a weaker rupee, heavy taxes and lingering geopolitical risks.

Sensex jumps 1,100 points: Why is stock market rising today?The benchmark BSE Sensex surged 892.05 points, or 1.18%, to 76,307.40 around 9:24 am, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 259.50 points, or 1.09%, to 23,978.80, moving closer to the key 24,000 mark.

Markets rally as crude slips under $100 amid US-Iran peace optimismThe benchmark BSE Sensex surged 892.05 points, or 1.18%, to 76,307.40 around 9:24 am, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 259.50 points, or 1.09%, to 23,978.80, moving closer to the key 24,000 mark.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market fall on petrol, diesel price hike?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,915 around 8:42 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty50 could open above Friday's closing level of 23,719.30.

Petrol prices hiked by nearly Rs 7.5/litre in two weeks: Check ratesThe repeated hikes in petrol and diesel prices come as India faces mounting pressure from elevated global crude oil prices linked to the ongoing West Asia conflict and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important oil shipping routes.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked again for 4th time; fuel up Rs 7.5 in under 2 weeksAnalysts have cautioned that continued hikes in petrol and diesel rates could raise transportation and logistics expenses, eventually leading to an increase in the prices of essential commodities and food products.

India's affordable housing dream hits a luxury roadblockIndia's housing market is tilting towards luxury projects even as affordable demand remains strong. Experts say rising costs, weak funding and policy gaps are pushing home ownership further from the middle class.

Would you wait 5 years for gratuity or switch for better pay? Indians speakSome career decisions are never easy, especially when timing complicates everything. Imagine being just months away from gratuity when a tempting job offer lands in your inbox. Do you stay a little longer or switch for better pay? I spoke to Indian professionals, and their answers were surprising.

Insurers cash in on unlimited health insurance, leave fine print to customersHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the second part of The Unlimited Illusion, we examine what insurers really mean by "unlimited" health cover and why customers often discover the limits only later.

entertainment.headline: Actor Khushbu Sundar felicitates Tamil Nadu CM Vijay, calls it emotional moment Actor-politician Khushbu Sundar met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay at the Secretariat during a STEPS delegation visit, describing the moment as unexpectedly emotional.

Emotional Hema Malini accepts Dharmendra's posthumous Padma Vibhushan. Video Hema Malini accepted the posthumous Padma Vibhushan awarded to Dharmendra from President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan. She was visibly emotional while receiving the honour on behalf of her late husband.

Fans mob Ram Charan at Peddi event in Bengaluru, pull injured hand Ram Charan was mobbed by fans during the Peddi pre-release press meet in Bengaluru. The rush reportedly re-injured the same hand he had previously hurt while shooting the film.

Brown teaser unveils Karisma Kapoor as troubled cop in psychological thriller Actor Karisma Kapoor returns to screens with the teaser of Brown, revealing her intense new role as a Kolkata cop in a dark psychological crime thriller.

Ranveer Singh reacts after industry ban amid Don 3 fallout with Farhan Akhtar The Don 3 controversy has taken a serious turn with FWICE banning Ranveer Singh over his reported exit from the film. Amid mounting pressure, the actor has finally responded -- maintaining silence, wishing the franchise success and refusing to fuel public speculation.

Ranveer Singh ban after Farhan Akhtar's Don 3 complaint: Tracing all that happened Film body FWICE has banned Ranveer Singh after Farhan Akhtar complained about the actor's reported exit from Don 3. How did it all start, and what happened down the years?

India's pop girl band era returns as VIVA's music hits streaming platforms VIVA's iconic music catalogue is finally back on streaming platforms after two decades, bringing renewed attention to India's first all-girl pop band and the era that shaped millennial pop culture.

Aishwarya Rai to Ella Bright, why women cannot escape body shaming From Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's Cannes scrutiny to the backlash against Off-Campus actor Ella Bright, here's why the internet's obsession with ageing, thinness and women's bodies reveals impossible beauty standards.

Big boost for Kollywood: CM Vijay allows 5 daily shows in Tamil Nadu theatres Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has allowed theatres to run five daily shows for seven days from a new Tamil film's release. The order is expected to lift opening-week business and has excited theatre owners.

Ahana Deol breaks down as Hema Malini accepts late Dharmendra's Padma Vibhushan Daughter Ahana Deol broke down as Hema Malini accepted the posthumous Padma Vibhushan for the late actor Dharmendra. The ceremony was held in New Delhi on May 25.

Ranveer Singh faces industry-wide ban as Don 3 fallout escalates Actor Ranveer Singh has been banned by the Federation of Western India Cine Employees in India following Farhan Akhtar's complaint in the Don 3 row.

3 brownies, 2 pizzas a day: Sara Ali Khan's diet before dramatic 45 kg weight loss Actor Sara Ali Khan revealed details about her eating habits before losing nearly 45 kg ahead of Kedarnath. From pizzas to brownies, the actor recalled her "mindless eating" phase during her student days in New York.

The 'wild' Deepika Padukone behind the 'good girl' image, ft. Imtiaz Ali Imtiaz Ali said he asked Deepika Padukone to read for Veronica in Cocktail instead of Meera. He also recalled how an unplanned silent Tamasha scene in Corsica came together on instinct.

Ram Charan's Peddi song faces heat over vulgar dance feat. Shruti Haasan, Janhvi Ram Charan, Janhvi Kapoor and the Peddi team launched Hallallallo song at a Bhopal event a couple of days ago. The song's dance visuals, featuring Shruti Haasan and Janhvi Kapoor, later drew online criticism over vulgarity and relevance.

Padma Awards 2026: When and where to watch the ceremony, full list of honourees President Droupadi Murmu will present the first set of Padma Awards 2026 at Rashtrapati Bhavan on Monday. The ceremony brings together major names such as the late Dharmendra, Mammootty, Alka Yagnik and R Madhavan.

Mohanlal flags drug menace in Keralam at Chief Minister VD Satheesan's felicitation At a felicitation event for Keralam Chief Minister VD Satheesan, Mohanlal warned that drugs had reached the gates of children's schools. He urged the government to act with an iron fist and said it had the strength to do so.

Malaika Arora steps out with rumoured boyfriend Harsh Mehta; son Arhaan joins. Video Malaika Arora was seen stepping out with Harsh Mehta and son Arhaan Khan. Harsh picked up Malaika from her mother's residence.

Drishyam 3 box office, budget and why the Mohanlal film didn't cross over this time Mohanlal's Drishyam 3 has opened strongly at the box office, with Kerala leading the film's four-day haul. The figures show robust franchise loyalty at home, even as its broader crossover pull looks limited.

Akshay Kumar, Akshara Singh go Ghis Ghis in Welcome 3 song, fans say garda uda diye Welcome To The Jungle has released its new song Ghis Ghis Ghis featuring Akshay Kumar and Akshara Singh. The track blends Bollywood and Bhojpuri flavours and has drawn upbeat reactions from viewers.

general news.headline: Siddaramaiah, DK Shivakumar in Delhi: 3 scenarios for Congress's Karnataka resetThe Congress leadership is weighing a political reset in Karnataka ahead of the 2028 Assembly elections, as crucial talks are scheduled in Delhi.

Ranveer Singh reacts after industry ban amid Don 3 fallout with Farhan AkhtarThe Don 3 controversy has taken a serious turn with FWICE banning Ranveer Singh over his reported exit from the film. Amid mounting pressure, the actor has finally responded -- maintaining silence, wishing the franchise success and refusing to fuel public speculation.

Trump's Abraham Accords invite to Asim Munir sparks embarrassment for Pakistan PMIn a lengthy Truth Social post about expanding the Abraham Accords after the Iran peace deal, Trump listed the world leaders he had spoken to over the weekend. But when it came to Pakistan, he did not mention Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif.

Iran President orders restoration of internet access after 87-day blackoutThe order was confirmed by officials from Iran's Communications Ministry, with state-linked outlets including Mehr News Agency reporting that Masoud Pezeshkian had issued a formal decree to reopen internet access.

The Samarkand Syndrome: Why Babur and family never became 'us'For the Timurids, or the Mughals of Hindustan, Samarkand remained their home, which they lost and could never get back. Bharat became the stage upon which their grandeur unfolded, while their imagination repeatedly drifted beyond the Hindu Kush toward the orchards, graves and memories of their homeland in Central Asia.
Rubio asked about Trump's hellhole remark against India. What he saidMarco Rubio sidestepped questions over Donald Trump's endorsement of "hellhole" remark about India and Indians.

Even babus can't save Delhi Gymkhana from its old boys' club imageAs Delhi Gymkhana fights eviction from prime Lutyens' land, the row has reignited a larger debate on whether public land should continue subsidising hereditary privilege and elite exclusivity in modern India.

3 AIADMK MLAs resign, switch to Vijay's TVK in fresh setback for EPSAIADMK MLAs Maragatham Kumaravel, Jayakumar and Sathyabama resigned from the Tamil Nadu Assembly and joined Chief Minister Vijay-led TVK.

Emotional Hema Malini accepts Dharmendra's posthumous Padma Vibhushan. VideoHema Malini accepted the posthumous Padma Vibhushan awarded to Dharmendra from President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan. She was visibly emotional while receiving the honour on behalf of her late husband.

Should be mandatory: Trump wants Saudi, Qatar, Pak to join Abraham AccordsDonald Trump said he urged leaders from several Muslim-majority nations to sign the Abraham Accords during high-level talks on Iran. He also warned that failure to secure a deal with Tehran could push the region back towards a far bigger conflict.

Provide proof, stay in US: Indians with H1-B visa may not need to panicA new US immigration clarification has brought relief to thousands of Indians on H-1B visas after fears grew that green card applicants may be forced to leave America. USCIS now says many workers providing economic benefit or serving national interest could still remain in the US during the process.

Storm brewing in TMC? Mamata's aide Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar quits party postKakoli Ghosh Dastidar, Trinamool Congress's Barasat MP and Mamata Banerjee's longtime ally, has resigned from her party post. Ghosh Dastidar blamed misgovernance within the TMC and the I-PAC for TMC's washout. Her resignation and subsequent attack by TMC's Kalyan Banerjee reveal the storm brewing within the party.

Ranveer Singh ban after Farhan Akhtar's Don 3 complaint: Tracing all that happenedFilm body FWICE has banned Ranveer Singh after Farhan Akhtar complained about the actor's reported exit from Don 3. How did it all start, and what happened down the years?

Heat, dust to remain in weather across north, rain to dominate KeralaOn Monday, the heat was relentless in the North. In the northeast and along the southern coast, it was raining hard enough to flood roads. The day continued 2026's run of intense, erratic weather, and Tuesday promises the same.

If only he knew history...: Iran's swipe at Marco Rubio after Taj Mahal visitUS Secretary of State Marco Rubio's Taj Mahal visit sparked a diplomatic jibe from the Iranian consulate in Hyderabad, which invoked the Mughal monument's Persian roots.

Only crime we are Shia: Pakistanis deported from the UAE amid Iran warBetween the US-Iran war and the fracturing of ties between the UAE and Pakistan, the Pakistani Shia diaspora in the Emirate has become the grass trampled underfoot. Thousands of Shia Pakistanis face deportation, even as a Sunni-majority UAE faces unprovoked attacks from a Shia Iran and Islamabad's mediation bid.

Video of cop laughing at press meet over Coimbatore girl's murder sparks outrageA video showing senior Tamil Nadu police officers smiling during a press conference on the murder of a 10-year-old girl in Coimbatore has sparked massive outrage online, with critics accusing the administration of insensitivity and demanding accountability.

Delhi's power and water crisis, summer 2026: A city on the boilA severe heatwave has pushed Delhi into simultaneous power and water stress. The strain is testing civic infrastructure and the Rekha Gupta government's summer response.

Was this toughest UPSC Prelims ever? Even bureaucrats struggled to solve paperMore than eight lakh aspirants appeared for the first level of one of India's most competitive and coveted recruitment tests, the Civil Services exam. Many aspirants and mentors are saying this was the toughest paper ever. They are asking if the UPSC questions have become exhaustive, or simply more unpredictable.

CBSE reaches out to Class 12 student after viral post on answer sheet mismatchCBSE has taken up Class 12 student Vedant's complaint over his Physics answer sheet on top priority after his post went viral. The case has put the board's post-result verification and re-evaluation process under fresh scrutiny.

UPSC Prelims paper absurd? Solve these 5 questions in 6 minutes to find outUPSC aspirants and mentors say this year's General Studies Paper 1 was unusually tough, long and packed with questions that should have been part of the Mains or the analytical CSAT. Before judging the outrage, give yourself six minutes and try answering these five questions from the paper.

Divorced daughter better than a dead one: MP government on Twisha Sharma caseThe Supreme Court said an independent agency should investigate Twisha Sharma's death and backed the Madhya Pradesh government's move for a CBI probe.

Who is Nishesh Basavareddy? 21-year-old Indian-origin man ousts Fritz at French OpenFrench Open: Nishesh Basavareddy, a 21-year-old American wildcard of Indian origin, announced himself on one of tennis' biggest stages after stunning seventh seed Taylor Fritz in the opening round of the Roland Garros on Sunday.

Rain coming: Satellite shows big cloud surge, yellow alert in these statesA western disturbance visible on INSAT-3DS imagery has triggered yellow alerts across the Himalayan states. The same satellite view also shows strengthening monsoon signals near Kerala, pointing to an early onset window.

sports.headline: Ruud survives five-set epic at French Open, Shelton also advances as Wawrinka bows out French Open: Casper Ruud survived Roman Safiullin in five sets at Roland Garros after blowing five match points and battling dizziness, cramps and heat stress. The escape kept his Paris campaign alive on a draining day that also brought wins for Amanda Anisimova and Ben Shelton, and a farewell for Stan Wawrinka.

No issues with Iran basing in Mexico for World Cup: President Claudia approves FIFA World Cup: Mexico will host Iran's World Cup 2026 base camp in Tijuana after the team was moved out of the United States. The switch shows how geopolitics and security concerns are already shaping tournament logistics.

Animesh Kujur wins 200m gold but misses CWG cut by 0.03s Animesh Kujur won the men's 200m final at the Federation Cup in Ranchi but missed the Commonwealth Games qualifying mark by three hundredths of a second. The result followed Gurindervir Singh taking away his 100m national record, deepening a dramatic week in Indian sprinting.

Shreyas Iyer's sister hits back at online abuse after PBKS playoff exit IPL 2026: Shresta Iyer has answered online abuse that resurfaced after Punjab Kings crashed out and an old PBKS-KKR banter reel went viral again. Her response widened into a sharp warning about cricket fandom dragging families, colleagues and workplaces into the firing line.

GT bowling attack stronger than RCB's: Sehwag names Qualifier 1 X-factor IPL 2026: Virender Sehwag has said Gujarat Titans carry a stronger bowling attack than Royal Challengers Bengaluru into Qualifier 1. His warning follows RCB's expensive outing against Sunrisers Hyderabad and sharpens focus on how both attacks handle playoff pressure.

Who after Neeraj Chopra? Federation Cup javelin final raises concern Shivam Lohakare won the Federation Cup men's javelin title in Ranchi with 81.71m, but no Indian met the Commonwealth Games qualifying mark. The result put the focus on India's limited depth beyond Neeraj Chopra ahead of the next major cycle.

Will Phil Salt play in RCB vs GT, Qualifier 1? Rajat Patidar shares update IPL 2026: Rajat Patidar said Phil Salt is fit and back in drills ahead of RCB's Qualifier 1 against Gujarat Titans. His return could restore Bengaluru's preferred batting order for a direct shot at the final.

Need to stay humble: Iga Swiatek after cruising through French Open first-round French Open 2026: Iga Swiatek brushed aside debutant Emerson Jones 6-1, 6-2 in just one hour at Roland Garros. The efficient start, despite a brief finger blister concern, came amid her coaching change and a titleless clay season.

Siraj x Rabada: GT pace duo carry huge hopes into Qualifier 1 vs RCB Vijay Dahiya said Mohammed Siraj and Kagiso Rabada have formed a strong bowling partnership for Gujarat Titans ahead of Qualifier 1 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Their powerplay impact has shaped GT's campaign and sets up a key pace battle in Dharamsala.

Why Rohit Sharma missed the Padma Shri ceremony in Delhi Rohit Sharma's absence from the Padma Shri ceremony in New Delhi surprised many fans, but the former India captain has not missed out on the honour itself. The Mumbai Indians star is expected to receive India's fourth-highest civilian award during a later investiture ceremony at Rashtrapati Bhavan.

RCB power-hitter Tim David to make comeback for Australia in 2027 ODI World Cup? Andrew McDonald has reopened the ODI door for Tim David after two retirements. Australia need middle-order finishing power, but David must commit to bilateral cricket before any World Cup push.

World Cup hero Harmanpreet Kaur receives Padma Shri, stuns in ethnic look: VideoPresident Droupadi Murmu presented the Padma Shri to Harmanpreet Kaur at Rashtrapati Bhavan on Monday. The honour recognises her contribution to Indian cricket ahead of the Women's T20 World Cup.

Shubman Gill, Sai Sudharsan best ever IPL batting pair: Suresh RainaSpain finally announced their FIFA World Cup 2026 squad, but the biggest talking point quickly became the complete absence of Real Madrid players. Euro 2024 champions Spain head into the tournament as one of the favourites, with Barcelona stars dominating Luis de la Fuente's squad.

Olympic champion Zheng Qinwen stunned by Poland youngster in major upsetFrench Open 2026: Maja Chwalinska produced a major Roland Garros upset by defeating Olympic champion Zheng Qinwen 6-4, 6-0 in the first round, storming into the second round on her main-draw debut in the tournament.

Spain pick World Cup squad with no Real Madrid playersSpain have named a 26-man World Cup squad without a single Real Madrid player, despite expectations around Dean Huijsen, Alvaro Carreras and Fran Garcia. The Barcelona-heavy selection has triggered strong reaction among supporters as Luis de la Fuente backs continuity before the Group H campaign.

Magnus Carlsen says others can chase World No. 1 as his chess priorities shiftAt Norway Chess, Magnus Carlsen said he now prefers a steadier mix weighted towards rapid and blitz over the grind of classical chess. The top-ranked Norwegian also argued the sport needs faster, tougher formats that create mistakes instead of preserving stale orthodoxy.

Flying Sikh 2.0 Gurindervir Singh leaves Anand Mahindra stunned with record runAnand Mahindra hailed Gurindervir Singh as a new hero of Indian sprinting after the Punjab athlete broke the men's 100m national record twice in two days in Ranchi, finally clocking 10.09 seconds to become India's fastest man.

Canadian GP: Hamilton claims Ferrari breakthrough, Antonelli creates F1 historyKimi Antonelli won the Canadian Grand Prix after George Russell retired, while Lewis Hamilton finished second for Ferrari. Antonelli's run extended his early-season dominance as Hamilton said Ferrari's changes finally made him comfortable.

Vaibhav wants first 200 in T20s, dreams of surpassing Chris Gayle's featVaibhav Sooryavanshi wants to become the first batter to score 200 in T20s. The target also reflects his wish to go past Chris Gayle's feat.

Ajinkya Rahane stays firm on captaincy after Kolkata's exit: I never back downAjinkya Rahane defended his captaincy despite Kolkata Knight Riders crashing out of the tournament. KKR stayed alive until the last day before Rajasthan's win over Mumbai at the Wankhede knocked them out.

Day 2 Highlights: Ruud, Swiatek, Shelton advance to next round as Wawrinka waves goodbyeFrench Open Day 2 Highlights: Competing in his final French Open, an emotional Stan Wawrinka waved goodbye to French open after first round loss to Jesper de Jong in four-sets (3-6, 6-3, 3-6, 4-6). Iga Swiatek, Ben Shelton, Elena Rybakina, and Amanda Anisimova proceeded to next rounds after dominating wins. While Casper Ruud overcame Roman Safiullin in a thrilling five-set battle.

'Nishesh, Nishesh' echoes at Roland Garros after Indian-origin star's 1st round winFrench Open 2026: Indian-origin Nishesh Basavareddy stunned No. 7 seed Taylor Fritz at Roland Garros, then won over the Paris crowd with a French speech after sealing the biggest win of his young career.

Doping Olympics' sprinters miss Bolt's record, clean athletes win despite oddsThe inaugural Enhanced Games in Las Vegas promised a superhuman, drug-fuelled show. Instead, clean athletes gatecrashed the party, leaving doping advocates with an awkward reality check.

Australia pace legend set to retire from ICC events after Women's T20 World CupAustralian fast bowler Megan Schutt has confirmed that the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup in England and Wales will be her final ICC tournament, bringing the curtain down on a decorated global career.

Vidit Gujrathi storms into Esports Chess World Cup Qualifier finalVidit Gujrathi reached the grand final of the India Rising: Road to EWC qualifiers and is now one win away from securing a spot at the 2026 Esports World Cup chess event.

Novak Djokovic challenges Aryna Sabalenka with special dance moveFrench Open 2026: Novak Djokovic revived his viral battle dance celebration and playfully challenged Aryna Sabalenka after his victory over Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard on Sunday, May 24.

Axar Patel backs SRH to win IPL 2026 title: That top 3 is formidableDelhi Capitals captain Axar Patel backs SunRisers Hyderabad to win the IPL this season. Axar said that if the top 3 click for SRH it might be difficult to stop the team from a title charge.

After Virat Kohli spat, Travis Head's wife reveals facing hate, brutal online abuseJessica Head revealed she faced fresh online abuse and trolling after Travis Head's on-field spat with Virat Kohli during the IPL 2026 clash between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

How India treats its champions: Record-breakers left hauling poles on e-rickshawHours after shattering the national record with identical 5.45m leaps at the 2026 Federation Cup, a viral video exposed India's top pole vaulters Dev Meena and Kuldeep Kumar hauling their 5m poles on a cramped e-rickshaw.

Day 2 schedule: Iga Swiatek, Elena Rybakina aim strong startDay 2 of the French Open 2026 brings a packed slate of opening-round action with top stars beginning their campaigns across all three show courts. Iga Swiątek and Elena Rybakina headline the action on Centre Court as they launch their title bids in Paris.

Criticised, trolled, not fully fit: Riyan still dares to chase Warne's RR legacyIPL 2026: Heavily criticised for both his on-field and off-field decisions, Riyan Parag battled injury to lead Rajasthan Royals into the playoffs. Now, he has the chance to match Shane Warne's legacy by becoming the first RR captain since 2008 to lift the IPL trophy and deliver Rajasthan's second title in franchise history.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
